title: cistus polymorphus proles villosus
intro: Cistus polymorphus proles villosus, also known as hairy rockrose, is a shrubby perennial plant species within the cistus genus, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems, and vibrant pink or white flowers. Traditionally, hairy rockrose has been used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties have been attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe wounds, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it beneficial for treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. Hairy rockrose can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
